title: SSO login redirect loop for users with plus-addressed emails or Entra ID aliases after Okta-to-Entra migration
problem: After migrating from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users experience an infinite redirect loop when logging into TaskFlow. Users authenticate successfully with Microsoft but are immediately redirected back to the identity provider upon returning to TaskFlow. Affected users can log into other SSO-integrated apps without issue.
root_cause_hypothesis: TaskFlow is likely matching the email claim from the Entra ID token against its stored user records using an exact string comparison. Users with '+' in their email (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) or users whose Entra ID primary email differs from the email originally used to create their TaskFlow account fail this match. When TaskFlow cannot associate the authenticated identity with a local user, it treats the session as unauthenticated and re-initiates the SSO flow, causing the loop.
reproduction_steps: Set up TaskFlow v2.3.1 (self-hosted) with Microsoft Entra ID SSO
Create a user in TaskFlow with a plus-addressed email (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) or with an email that differs from their Entra ID primary email
Attempt to log in as that user via SSO
Observe the infinite redirect loop: TaskFlow → Microsoft login → successful auth → TaskFlow → Microsoft login → ...
environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1, self-hosted. Mixed client environments (Chrome/Edge/Firefox on Windows 10/11 and macOS). Recently migrated SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID.
impact: Approximately 30% of the team is completely unable to log into TaskFlow. This is a total access blocker for affected users with no known workaround.
recommended_fix: Investigate the SSO callback handler where TaskFlow matches the identity token's email claim to a local user record. Check whether: (1) plus-addressed emails are being normalized or matched correctly, (2) the email claim from Entra ID tokens matches what TaskFlow has stored (Okta may have sent a different claim format than Entra ID), (3) alias emails are checked in addition to primary emails. Consider normalizing email comparison (case-insensitive, plus-address stripping) or matching on a more stable identifier like the OIDC 'sub' claim rather than email alone.
proposed_test_case: Write an integration test for the SSO callback that authenticates with a token containing a plus-addressed email (user+tag@domain.com) and verifies the user is matched to the correct account and logged in successfully, rather than redirected back to the IdP.
information_gaps: Exact email claim(s) Entra ID is returning in the token vs. what TaskFlow has stored for affected users
Server-side logs from TaskFlow during a failed login attempt (would confirm whether the match failure is the cause)
Whether Okta was configured to send a different email claim format than Entra ID defaults to
